summary: Official user policy surfaces for disabling AutoPlay. Microsoft documents TurnOffAutoPlay through NoDriveTypeAutoRun under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer and documents DisallowAutoplayForNonVolumeDevices through HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\NoAutoplayfornonVolume. The app now writes both documented user-scope policy values directly.

setting.name: Disable AutoPlay
setting.casual_explanation: AutoPlay decides what Windows does when media or devices are connected. Turning it off stops Windows from automatically offering or launching actions when drives, cameras, or phones appear.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable AutoPlay to reduce surprise popups, avoid accidental actions when removable media is connected, and keep device handling more manual.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning AutoPlay off can make device handling calmer and safer, but it removes convenience when you expect Windows to react automatically to inserted media or connected devices.
setting.professional_notes: Local AutoPlay.admx and AutoPlay.adml document two official policy surfaces relevant here. Turn off Autoplay is stored as NoDriveTypeAutoRun under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer, with the All drives option encoded as decimal 255. Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices is stored as NoAutoplayfornonVolume under HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The app now writes both official user-scope policy values directly.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps its normal AutoPlay behavior for drive types not otherwise changed by policy.

allowed_values.label: Turn off AutoPlay on CD-ROM and removable media drives
allowed_values.meaning: AutoPlay is turned off on CD-ROM and removable media drives only.

allowed_values.label: Turn off AutoPlay on all drives
allowed_values.meaning: AutoPlay is turned off on all drives.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps its normal behavior for non-volume devices such as cameras or phones.

allowed_values.label: Non-volume AutoPlay allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and AutoPlay is enabled for non-volume devices.

allowed_values.label: Non-volume AutoPlay turned off
allowed_values.meaning: AutoPlay is blocked for non-volume devices such as cameras or phones.

app_current_implementation.notes: The provider now writes the documented user-scope AutoPlay policy values directly: NoDriveTypeAutoRun = 255 and NoAutoplayfornonVolume = 1.

windows_defaults.label: Windows AutoPlay policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Supported Windows editions exposing the AutoPlay policy surfaces

states.rationale: Leave the official AutoPlay drive policy unset.
Leave the official non-volume device policy unset.

windows_defaults.notes: Windows keeps normal AutoPlay behavior when these user policies are not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable AutoPlay
intended_for: Users who want manual handling for inserted media and connected devices
Users who want fewer automatic prompts when devices arrive
avoid_for: Users who rely on Windows to react automatically to media insertion

states.rationale: Turn off AutoPlay on all drives with the documented All drives enum value.
Block AutoPlay for non-volume devices such as cameras and phones.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a policy-backed AutoPlay off state for drives and non-volume devices.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You expect Windows to open or offer device actions automatically.
tradeoffs: Windows stops offering normal AutoPlay reactions for drives and non-volume devices.
This reduces automatic prompts and surprise actions.
Reversible by deleting the values or returning them to their disabled states.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want normal AutoPlay behavior
avoid_for: Users who want a strict policy-backed AutoPlay block

states.rationale: Leave the official AutoPlay policies unset.
Leave the official AutoPlay policies unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep normal device-arrival behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a formal AutoPlay block.
tradeoffs: Device arrival actions can still be offered normally.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: AutoPlay / TurnOffAutoPlay
evidence.summary: Official Microsoft documentation for the policy that turns AutoPlay off.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AutoPlay.admx TurnOffAutoPlay mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps Turn off Autoplay to HKCU or HKLM Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\NoDriveTypeAutoRun, with enum value 181 for CD-ROM and removable media drives and 255 for All drives.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AutoPlay.adml TurnOffAutoPlay help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says the policy allows turning off AutoPlay on CD-ROM and removable media drives or on all drives.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: AutoPlay / DisallowAutoplayForNonVolumeDevices
evidence.summary: Official Microsoft documentation for the policy that disallows AutoPlay for non-volume devices such as cameras or phones.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AutoPlay.admx NoAutoplayfornonVolume mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps NoAutoplayfornonVolume to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AutoPlay.adml NoAutoplayfornonVolume help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy disallows AutoPlay for MTP devices like cameras or phones.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoDriveTypeAutoRun = 255 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer and NoAutoplayfornonVolume = 1 under HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the official AutoPlay policy surfaces, and local ADMX and ADML files publish the exact registry paths and numeric states used to turn AutoPlay off on all drives and for non-volume devices. The app now writes those documented user-scope policy values directly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="Autorun" class="Both" key="Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer"> <enum id="Autorun_Box" valueName="NoDriveTypeAutoRun"> <item displayName="$(string.Autorun_None)"><value><decimal value="255" /></value></item>; <policy name="NoAutoplayfornonVolume" class="Both" key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer" valueName="NoAutoplayfornonVolume"> <enabledValue><decimal value="1" /></enabledValue><disabledValue><decimal value="0" /></disabledValue>
validation_proof.notes: Local official AutoPlay.admx publishes the exact registry paths and numeric states for the two policy surfaces used by this tweak.
